get.description:
The systemOptimize method GET will return system details such as TSRF, SAV, module count, annual AC production, and DC size.
The information will also be available by mounting plane or facet.
If working with Solar Tiles, active versus passive tile count will be available.
Output for this method include JSON and PDF.

The systemOptimize method PUT will increase or decrease the system for the specified reportId and versionId for the following parameters:TSRF, Module Count, Active Tile Count, DC Size, and AC Production.
Multiple parameters can be use in a signle request based on the order they are listed in the request body.

info.description:
# Introduction ## About TrueDesign API provides access to solar design data, enabling seamless integration with various tools and platforms.
[Product Availability](#tag/Product-Availability) retrieves the availability of TrueDesign products for a given geo location. [Launch](#tag/Launch) initiates a TrueDesign product for a specified geo location. [Settings](#tag/Settings) retrieves or updates presets settings for a TrueDesign product. [Optimization](#tag/Optimization) enhances solar design by adjusting panel layout for maximum efficiency. [Management](#tag/Management) handles version actions such as updates, status checks, rename and deletions. [Exports](#tag/Exports) generates and retrieves TrueDesign product deliverables in various formats. [Roof](#tag/Roof) provides roof summary data and control facets for a given geo location.
Refer to the [TrueDesign API User Guide](/user-guides/developer-guides/truedesign-api-&-sdk) for detailed implementation guidance. ## Documentation Overview Each API endpoint in this documentation consists of: - **The HTTP method.** Currently includes `GET`, `DELETE`, `PUT`, `PATCH` and `POST`. - **The base path.** The base path is `https://sandbox.apicenter.eagleview.com` for the sandbox or `https://apicenter.eagleview.com` for production. This base path is specified before the endpoint path. - **The endpoint path.** For example, `/solar/v1/truedesign/productAvailability/available`. - **Required parameters.** These parameters must be included in a request. In the previous example, `Address` is a required parameter. - **Optional parameters.** These parameters can be included in a request if desired. # Authentication
info.title: TrueDesign™ API Documentation

tags.name: Product Availability
tags.description: The Product Availability API allows you to check to see if Eagleview has the required imagery to process one or more Solar Products. This process only checks if imagery is available and only after an order is placed can Eagleview determine if the imagery if sufficent enough for the request product.

tags.name: Launch
tags.description: TrueDesign is an iFrame application and this API will allow you to initialize your session with varying degrees of customization.

tags.name: Settings
tags.description: TrueDesign's Settings APIs allow to know and control the presets being applied to your design.

tags.name: Optimization
tags.description: TrueDesign's Optimization APIs enables you to access current System Details based on the PV layout and make changes like adjusting to production (kWh) offsets, system size (kW), module count (#), or minimum TSRF (%). You can also apply new max layouts based on design criteria like module orientation or alignment.

tags.name: Management
tags.description: TrueDesign's Management APIs allow you to control different system design versions like create, copy, delete, and list.

tags.name: Exports
tags.description: TrueDesign's Export APIs allow you to retrieve data, images, reports, and CAD files from your design.

tags.name: Roof
tags.description: Truedesign's Roof API allows you to retrieve roof summary data and control facets (mounting planes) that can support design.

reportId.description: Unique id generated by EagleView when an order is placed.

vintageExtension.description: For orders that contain solar shade analysis. True allows for imagery up to 8 years old. False allows for imagery up to 4 years old.
